overall_assessment: The bounded package is directionally closure-ready for a verification-debt rerun because it explicitly records the remaining blockers, distinguishes current proof from pending audit lanes, and avoids claiming RR-AUTH-04 is already closable. Residual debt still exists in the acknowledged missing fail-first provenance, so final acceptance should continue to rely on the substitute evidence map and the still-pending rerun artifacts rather than imply fully preserved red-to-green history.
recommended_path: Keep RR-AUTH-04 blocked for closure, use this packet as bounded rerun evidence only, and preserve explicit language that the missing fail-first artifacts remain accepted historical verification debt until the pending rerun review lanes, triple-audit round 02, Claude comparison, and orchestration guards are completed.

findings.title: Historical fail-first provenance remains unresolved residual verification debt
findings.rationale: The package explicitly states that preserved fail-first/red-run artifacts are unavailable and treats the assertion/evidence map plus rerun validation as the substitute proof surface. That is acceptable for bounded rerun review, but it remains real verification debt and would become overclaim if any later closure language implied full preserved TDD provenance instead of acknowledged substitute evidence.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the accepted-debt wording explicit in the acceptance ledger and any closure summary, and avoid language that suggests preserved fail-first history exists for RR-AUTH-04.
